Kropz plc (AIM: KRPZ), an emerging African phosphate producer, is pleased to announce that it has received approval from the South African Reserve Bank ("
Exchange Control Approval

Consequently, the Fundraising is now wholly unconditional.
An application has been made for the 79,776,625 new Ordinary Shares ("New Ordinary Shares") pursuant to the Fundraising to be admitted to trading on AIM ("Admission"). Dealings in respect of the New Ordinary Shares are expected to commence on or around 8.00 a.m. on 6 May 2026.

latest.title: Operational Update
latest.content:
Today 08:44
RNS Number : 1144B
Kropz PLC
20 April 2026
20 April 2026
Kropz Plc
("
Kropz
" or the "
Company
")
Operational Update
Kropz Plc (AIM: KRPZ), an emerging African phosphate producer and developer, announces a trading update for the period to 31 March 2026.
Elandsfontein
The quarter and financial period closed with a record production month at Elandsfontein in March 2026, during which 40,792 tonnes of phosphate concentrate were produced.
As previously announced on 24 November 2025, September 2025 marked a production record of 34,070 tonnes. Since that announcement, four additional production records have been achieved during the final six months of the financial period ended 31 March 2026.
Elandsfontein produced 115,686 tonnes of phosphate concentrate in Q4 of the financial period ended 31 March 2026, representing a 11% increase compared to the previous quarter. Sales for the period totalled 104,900 tonnes.
The mine remains in its trial production phase.
Hinda Project
The Hinda Project has performed a review and assessment of alternative production scenarios, which involved comprehensive stress testing and other additional studies. As a result, the complexities associated with both surface and underground water have been significantly reduced and effectively mitigates the risk associated through optimisation initiative. Therefore, management no longer considers water-related challenges to pose a material risk, contributing to improved overall project profitability. This is a major milestone and repositions the entire feasibility of the project.

Result of WRAP Retail Offer
Results of WRAP Retail Offer and Update on Subscription
Kropz
plc (AIM:
KRPZ) today announces the result of its offer to its existing retail investors via the Winterflood Retail Access Platform (the "
WRAP
Retail Offer
") and the results of its conditional subscription with the ARC Fund ("
ARC
") (the "
Subscription
").
Retail investors have conditionally subscribed in the WRAP Retail Offer for a total of 299,640 ordinary shares of 0.1 pence each in the capital of the Company ("
Ordinary Shares
") (the "
WRAP
Retail Offer Shares
"). Consequently, ARC will subscribe for the remaining 7,620,153 ordinary shares under the Subscription in accordance with the underwriting arrangements (taking ARC's total subscription to 79,476,985 ordinary shares).
The Fundraising in aggregate comprised 79,776,625 New Ordinary Shares raising total gross proceeds of approximately £917,431 for the Company.
Exchange Control Approval and Admission
The issue of the total of 79,776,625 new ordinary shares (the "
New Ordinary Shares
") is conditional upon
approval from the South African Reserve Bank ("
Exchange Control Approval
")
and upon admission of the New Ordinary Shares to trading on AIM ("
Admission
") becoming effective.
Application will be made to London Stock Exchange plc for Admission, which, subject to
receipt of Exchange Control Approval, is expected to take place at 8.00 a.m.
on
26 March 2026, being the fourth business day following the anticipated date of Exchange Control Approval.
The date of Admission will be reconfirmed upon announcement of the receipt of Exchange Control Approval
.
Total Voting Rights
Immediately following Admission, the Company's issued share capital will be
1,647,367,866
Ordinary Shares, with each share carrying the right to one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total voting rights figure immediately following Admission, of
1,647,367,866
may be used by shareholders (and others with notification obligations) as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
The new Ordinary Shares to be issued pursuant to the WRAP Retail Offer will be issued free of all liens, charges and encumbrances and will, on Admission, rank
pari passu
in all respects with the new Ordinary Shares to be issued pursuant to the Placing, the Subscription and the Company's existing Ordinary Shares.

company_30s.what_they_do: KRPZ.L is a phosphate mining company that extracts and sells phosphate rock and related products for use in fertilizers and industrial applications.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Phosphate is a critical raw material for global agriculture, and the supply of high‑quality phosphate is limited. The company owns a 35‑year mine life and has secured a free‑float of 10%, positioning it to supply the growing demand for fertilizers, especially in emerging markets.
company_30s.current_state: The business is still in the early stages of production. It has been raising capital through wrap offers and bridge loans, and it recently received ECA approval for its share admission. Cash burn remains high, but the company is moving toward a stable operating model. The share price sits well below its historic highs, reflecting a deep decline.

rns_narrative: The company has been actively restructuring its debt, securing bridge financing and amending loan terms to keep the mine operational. This shows a clear intent to stay afloat while it prepares for production.
Operational updates confirm that the company has met regulatory milestones (ECA approval) and is working to bring the mine online. These milestones are key to unlocking future cash flow.

Governance and legal filings (AGM, director disclosure, legal claim updates) indicate a company that is compliant and actively managing its corporate affairs, suggesting a stable governance environment.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism: traders are whispering hope while the broader market remains silent.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices highlight the company's long mine life and the potential for a strong rebound once production starts. They see the recent funding and regulatory approvals as a green light for a turnaround.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter points to the heavy cash burn, the drawdown on bridge loans, and the fact that the company has yet to generate revenue. They fear the debt load could become unsustainable if production is delayed.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Initially cautious, sentiment has begun to lift after the ECA approval and the latest operational update, but remains muted because the share price is still far from its peak.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search interest, meaning the stock is hidden from the public eye and could surprise if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: A clear production start date announced in the next earnings release would validate the company’s operational plan and likely lift the share price.
An increase in trading volume that exceeds the 20‑day average by 150% would signal renewed investor interest and a possible bottom.
If the company announces a new strategic partnership or a significant supply contract for its phosphate, that would provide a tangible revenue source.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained uptick in volume, a bullish price move breaking the recent low, and any confirmation that the bridge loan has been fully drawn down. These would validate the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early capitulation phase—prices have fallen far below their peak and the market has largely ignored it. A sharp spike in volume or a positive operational announcement would likely trigger the next leg of the recovery.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash (well over 70%) has left a large upside if the company can start generating cash flow. Historical data shows that similar deep‑crash stocks have returned 100‑200% when a catalyst arrives. If KRPZ.L recovers to the midpoint between its trough and its all‑time high, the upside could be 150‑200%.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still faces significant cash burn and a heavy debt load. If production is delayed beyond the next quarter, the bridge loan could run out, forcing a liquidation or a drastic price collapse. The worst‑case downside is a 100% loss of the invested capital.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk: 100% of the invested capital (if the company fails to start production). Reward: 150‑200% upside if the share climbs to the midpoint of its historic range. The asymmetry is strongly in favor of the upside, making this a high‑reward, high‑risk play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter at the first significant volume spike that breaks the recent low. Allocate 10% of your portfolio to the position, use a trailing stop 20% below the entry price, and adjust the stop as the price moves higher.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a clear operational milestone—such as a production start date or a signed supply contract. Once that appears, look for a 30% price move above the recent low before buying.
playbook.if_passing: None. There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals; the company is still operational and compliant.

ai_insights.bottom_line: KRPZ.L sits at the bottom of a deep crash, but with a long mine life and recent regulatory approvals, a near‑term catalyst could unlock a 150‑200% upside, making it a compelling contrarian bet despite the 100% downside risk.
